categories.name: أَقَارِبُ الْأَنْبِيَاء
categories.nameEn: Relatives of Prophets mentioned in the Quran

items.verse: ۞ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱصْطَفَىٰٓ ءَادَمَ وَنُوحًۭا وَءَالَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَءَالَ عِمْرَٰنَ عَلَى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ
items.verseEn: Indeed, Allāh chose Adam and Noah and the family of Abraham and the family of ʿImrān over the worlds -

items.verse: وَٱذْكُرْ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مَرْيَمَ إِذِ ٱنتَبَذَتْ مِنْ أَهْلِهَا مَكَانًۭا شَرْقِيًّۭا
items.verseEn: And mention, [O Muḥammad], in the Book [the story of] Mary, when she withdrew from her family to a place toward the east.

items.verse: وَقَالَتِ ٱمْرَأَتُ فِرْعَوْنَ قُرَّتُ عَيْنٍۢ لِّى وَلَكَ ۖ لَا تَقْتُلُوهُ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَنفَعَنَآ أَوْ نَتَّخِذَهُۥ وَلَدًۭا وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
items.verseEn: And the wife of Pharaoh said, "[He will be] a comfort of the eye [i.e., pleasure] for me and for you. Do not kill him; perhaps he may benefit us, or we may adopt him as a son." And they perceived not.

categories.name: أَوْصَافٌ لِلْمَاءِ أَوْ مَصْدَرِهِ
categories.nameEn: Adjectival descriptions of water or its source

items.verse: ءَأَنتُمْ أَنزَلْتُمُوهُ مِنَ ٱلْمُزْنِ أَمْ نَحْنُ ٱلْمُنزِلُونَ
items.verseEn: Is it you who brought it down from the clouds, or is it We who bring it down?

items.verse: وَمَا يَسْتَوِى ٱلْبَحْرَانِ هَـٰذَا عَذْبٌۭ فُرَاتٌۭ سَآئِغٌۭ شَرَابُهُۥ وَهَـٰذَا مِلْحٌ أُجَاجٌۭ ۖ وَمِن كُلٍّۢ تَأْكُلُونَ لَحْمًۭا طَرِيًّۭا وَتَسْتَخْرِجُونَ حِلْيَةًۭ تَلْبَسُونَهَا ۖ وَتَرَى ٱلْفُلْكَ فِيهِ مَوَاخِرَ لِتَبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
items.verseEn: And not alike are the two seas [i.e., bodies of water]. One is fresh and sweet, palatable for drinking, and one is salty and bitter. And from each you eat tender meat and extract ornaments which you wear, and you see the ships plowing through [them] that you might seek of His bounty; and perhaps you will be grateful.

items.verse: أَوْ يُصْبِحَ مَآؤُهَا غَوْرًۭا فَلَن تَسْتَطِيعَ لَهُۥ طَلَبًۭا
items.verseEn: Or its water will become sunken [into the earth], so you would never be able to seek it."
And sent down, from the rain clouds, pouring water.

categories.name: أَفْعَالٌ مُتَعَلِّقَةٌ بِمَفْهُومِ النَّسْخِ
categories.nameEn: Verbs related to the Islamic concept of abrogation (Naskh)

items.verse: ۞ مَا نَنسَخْ مِنْ ءَايَةٍ أَوْ نُنسِهَا نَأْتِ بِخَيْرٍۢ مِّنْهَآ أَوْ مِثْلِهَآ ۗ أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ قَدِيرٌ
items.verseEn: We do not abrogate a verse or cause it to be forgotten except that We bring forth [one] better than it or similar to it. Do you not know that Allāh is over all things competent?

items.verse: يَمْحُوا۟ ٱللَّهُ مَا يَشَآءُ وَيُثْبِتُ ۖ وَعِندَهُۥٓ أُمُّ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ
items.verseEn: Allāh eliminates what He wills or confirms, and with Him is the Mother of the Book.

items.verse: وَإِذَا بَدَّلْنَآ ءَايَةًۭ مَّكَانَ ءَايَةٍۢ ۙ وَٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يُنَزِّلُ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مُفْتَرٍۭ ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
items.verseEn: And when We substitute a verse in place of a verse - and Allāh is most knowing of what He sends down - they say, "You, [O Muḥammad], are but an inventor [of lies]." But most of them do not know.
We will make you recite, [O Muḥammad], and you will not forget,

items.verse: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَنَا قَالَ يَـٰلَيْتَ بَيْنِى وَبَيْنَكَ بُعْدَ ٱلْمَشْرِقَيْنِ فَبِئْسَ ٱلْقَرِينُ
items.verseEn: Until, when he comes to Us [at Judgement], he says [to his companion], "How I wish there was between me and you the distance between the east and west; and what a wretched companion."
[He is] Lord of the two sunrises and Lord of the two sunsets.
And was at a distance of two bow lengths or nearer.
And that He creates the two mates - the male and female -

harf.hint: Described as the greatest of wrongs when associated with the Divine.
harf.arabicVerse: وَإِذْ قَالَ لُقْمَـٰنُ لِٱبْنِهِۦ وَهُوَ يَعِظُهُۥ يَـٰبُنَىَّ لَا تُشْرِكْ بِٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّ ٱلشِّرْكَ لَظُلْمٌ عَظِيمٌۭ
harf.verse: 31:13 — And [mention, O Muḥammad], when Luqmān said to his son while he was instructing him, "O my son, do not associate [anything] with Allāh. Indeed, association [with Him] is great injustice."

deduction.title: The Warning Amidst the Dunes
deduction.intro: A messenger was sent to a powerful and arrogant civilization, famed for their towering structures and immense physical strength. This individual came not with armies or wealth, but with a clear warning and a call back to the worship of the One True God. Despite the blessings bestowed upon them, the people met this call with ridicule and defiance, unknowingly sealing their own fate. Can you identify this soul and the story of their people's downfall?
clues: I was sent to my own brethren, a people of great physical strength and architectural skill.
They prided themselves on building lofty structures on every high place, yet I questioned the purpose of these monuments.
I reminded them of the blessings Allah had given them, like cattle and children, gardens and springs, but they accused me of falsehood.
My people challenged me directly, saying, 'Bring upon us what you threaten us with, if you are of the truthful.'
I warned them of a punishment that would appear as a vast cloud advancing toward their valleys, which they would mistake for life-giving rain.
I am the one who warned the people of 'Ad in the wind-swept sand-hills of Al-Ahqaf.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Salih
Prophet Shu'ayb
Prophet Hud
Prophet Nuh
Prophet Lut

trial.label: What was my primary trial?
options: Debating a king about who gives life and death
Calling the arrogant people of 'Ad to Tawhid
Leading an exodus away from a tyrannical Pharaoh
Building a massive ark to survive a global flood
Being swallowed by a giant fish as a test
trial.answer: Calling the arrogant people of 'Ad to Tawhid

location.label: What is a key element in my people's story?
options: A staff that turned into a serpent
A she-camel that emerged from a rock
A fire that was made cool and peaceful
A furious and destructive wind
A flock of birds carrying baked clay stones

outcome.label: What was the outcome for my people?
options: Annihilation by a wind for seven nights and eight days
Being turned into apes for violating the Sabbath
Drowning in the sea after a miraculous parting
Destruction by a mighty blast and earthquake
Being pelted by a rain of hard stones
outcome.answer: Annihilation by a wind for seven nights and eight days

deduction.arabic: ۞ وَٱذْكُرْ أَخَا عَادٍ إِذْ أَنذَرَ قَوْمَهُۥ بِٱلْأَحْقَافِ وَقَدْ خَلَتِ ٱلنُّذُرُ مِنۢ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِۦٓ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍۢ
deduction.verse: And mention, [O Muḥammad], the brother of ʿAad, when he warned his people in [the region of] al-Aḥqāf - and warners had already passed on before him and after him - [saying], "Do not worship except Allāh. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a terrible day." (46:21)

scramble.hint: A reminder of Allah's provision in this world and our ultimate return to Him.
scramble.arabic: هُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ ذَلُولًۭا فَٱمْشُوا۟ فِى مَنَاكِبِهَا وَكُلُوا۟ مِن رِّزْقِهِۦ ۖ وَإِلَيْهِ ٱلنُّشُورُ
scramble.verseEn: It is He who made the earth tame for you - so walk among its slopes and eat of His provision - and to Him is the resurrection.
